title: Xanthophyllum subcoriaceum
taxonomy: Xanthophyllum subcoriaceum belongs to the family Polygalaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as milkwort or bitter-pea family. This family comprises approximately 1,200 species spread across 85 genera. Xanthophyllum subcoriaceum is classified as a species within the genus Xanthophyllum. The genus Xanthophyllum itself is part of the Polygalales order and the flowering plant clade. Xanthophyllum subcoriaceum's classification is a part of the broader Angiosperm phylogeny.
uses: Xanthophyllum subcoriaceum has been reported to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat fever, headache, and other ailments. Its leaves are sometimes used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The plant's roots and leaves may also have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
